title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify branch/commit existence, whether commit 1c8023c7 is on feature/phase0-handoff-pipeline, and whether there is evidence it was pushed. Use local git evidence only unless remote check is necessary. Return concise findings. Do not edit files.
summary_text: **Highest-severity remaining risk:** [Any local actor can forge completion evidence for `codex`/`glm`/specialist lanes because `kernel-runtime-ledger.sh` allows unrestricted `record-provider` writes, and `kernel-runtime-health.sh` / `kernel-consensus-evidence.sh` accept those bare counters as truth (`success_count`) to grant healthy/approved states.** [\(\[kernel-runtime-ledger\]\(/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh:220\)-(\[273\])\), [\(\[kernel-runtime-health\]\(/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh:188\)-(\[262\])\), [\(\[kernel-consensus-evidence\]\(/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh:305\)-(\[330\])\)]. **Why this is provable from current tests/docs:** - Tests directly write synthetic success evidence via CLI and then assert health/consensus outcomes, e.g., `record-provider ... success` in [test-kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh:69-73), [test-kernel-consensus-evidence.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-consensus-evidence.sh:48-55), [test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:31-33), and [test-kernel-launch-working-orchestration.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-launch-working-orchestration.sh:65-67). - `kernel-launch-quorum.sh` itself does run lanes but its role is not to emit verifiable per-lane proof and it does not prevent synthetic post-facto ledger inflation in this chain [kernel-launch-quorum]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh:57-119). **Minimal fix strategy:** 1. Require `record-provider` (and transition state writes used for health/consensus) to accept only signed/attested evidence records (run-id/provider/exit-code/cmd fingerprint/artifact hash + nonce/HMAC), and reject plain positional CLI writes by default. 2. Gate raw `record-provider` with a protected mode flag, e.g., `KERNEL_RUNTIME_LEDGER_STRICT=true` in production and a documented test escape hatch (`KERNEL_RUNTIME_LEDGER_ALLOW_MANUAL=true`) for local tests. 3. Make health/consensus verify proof presence/validity before counting `success_count`, and optionally maintain an append-only event log/hash chain for ledger tamper detection.
